title: Lilium pardanthinum
intro: Lilium pardalinum, commonly known as the leopard lily, is a species of lily native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The health benefits of Lilium pardalinum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling, as well as protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Lilium pardalinum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lilium pardalinum include tinctures, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and musculoskeletal pain.

The order Liliales is a subgroup of the larger class Monocotyledoneae, which includes all flowering plants that have one cotyledon in their seeds.
